pattern: A single linear scan can track every independent strength rule at once, returning false the moment any requirement fails.
hints: Five conditions must all hold; you can verify each with one pass over the characters.
Track booleans for lowercase, uppercase, digit, and special-character presence, plus a length check and an adjacency check.
While scanning, flag the category each character belongs to, reject any character equal to its predecessor, and finally require length at least eight and all four category flags set.

edge_inputs.description: missing digit and has adjacent repeats
all categories present but two equal chars are adjacent

stress.description: random strings over the allowed character set, mostly invalid by chance
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randrange(1, max(2, min(size, 100)) + 1)
    pool = 'abcdefghijklmABCDEFGHIJKLM0123456789!@#$%^&*()-+'
    return [''.join(rng.choice(pool) for _ in range(n))]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def strongPasswordCheckerII(self, password):
        specials = set('!@#$%^&*()-+')
        if len(password) < 8:
            return False
        has_lower = any(c.islower() for c in password)
        has_upper = any(c.isupper() for c in password)
        has_digit = any(c.isdigit() for c in password)
        has_special = any(c in specials for c in password)
        no_adj = all(password[i] != password[i - 1] for i in range(1, len(password)))
        return has_lower and has_upper and has_digit and has_special and no_adj
